goal: Scan Google Docs in the Drive 'Contracts' folder, extract contract end/expiration dates, and email an HTML summary table of contracts ending within the next 30 days (plus documents where no end date was found) to specified recipients.

config.description: Name of the Google Drive folder containing contract documents
Number of days ahead to check for expiring contracts
Subject line for the summary email
config.default: Contracts ending in the next 30 days (Contracts folder)

invariants: Only Google Docs documents directly inside the Contracts folder are considered (no subfolders)
Dates must be normalized to YYYY-MM-DD format for comparison
Documents with no extractable end date must still appear in the report marked as 'End date not found'
Report is sorted: soonest-expiring first, then 'End date not found' items at the bottom
Email is always sent even if no contracts are expiring soon
required_outcomes: An HTML email is sent to the specified recipients containing a summary table of contracts ending within the configured window and contracts with missing end dates
Each included contract shows document name, document link, end date (or 'End date not found'), days remaining, and notes

steps.summary: Find the Google Drive folder named 'Contracts' (get existing folder)
List only Google Docs files directly inside the Contracts folder (non-recursive, filtered to Google Docs type)
For each Google Doc contract, read its content and extract end/expiration date information

do.summary: Read the full text content of the contract document
Extract the contract end/expiration date from the document text using label-aware scanning, normalize to YYYY-MM-DD, and handle ambiguity

generate.instruction: Analyze the document text content to find the contract end or expiration date. Look for labels such as 'End Date', 'Expiration Date', 'Expiry Date', 'Term ends', 'Termination Date', 'Valid until', and similar phrases. If multiple candidate end/expiration dates are found, prefer the date closest to the phrases 'End Date' or 'Expiration Date'. If still ambiguous, choose the latest date and set has_multiple_dates to true. Normalize the extracted date to YYYY-MM-DD format. If no end/expiration date can be found at all, set end_date to null and end_date_found to false. Also include the document name and webViewLink from the contract_doc metadata.
generate.reason: Requires semantic reasoning to identify date labels in unstructured contract text, resolve ambiguity between multiple candidate dates, and determine which date represents the contract end/expiration

outputs.name: document_name
outputs.description: Name of the contract document from metadata

outputs.name: document_link
outputs.description: Web view link to the document from metadata

outputs.name: end_date
outputs.description: Extracted end/expiration date in YYYY-MM-DD format, or null if not found

outputs.name: end_date_found
outputs.description: Whether an end/expiration date was successfully extracted

outputs.name: has_multiple_dates
outputs.description: Whether multiple candidate end/expiration dates were found in the document

outputs.name: notes
outputs.description: Any notes about the extraction, e.g. 'Multiple possible dates found' or empty string

steps.summary: Add days_remaining and date_window_end fields to each contract analysis result for filtering and display
Keep contracts where end date is within the next 30 days OR where no end date was found

transform.description: Include contracts ending within the configured window (days_remaining >= 0 and days_remaining <= days_window) OR contracts where no end date was found

steps.summary: Compute counts of expiring-soon contracts and missing-date contracts for the email header
Build the HTML email body with a summary header and a sorted table of reportable contracts (soonest-expiring first, missing-date items last)

generate.instruction: Generate a professional HTML email body for a contract expiration report. The email should include: 1) A brief header summary stating how many contracts are expiring within 30 days and how many have missing end dates (use the total_reportable count, expiring_soon_contracts count, and missing_date_contracts count). 2) If total_reportable is 0, display a clear message: 'No contracts ending in the next 30 days.' and skip the table. 3) If there are reportable contracts, build an HTML table with columns: Document Name (as a clickable hyperlink using document_link), End/Expiration Date (show 'End date not found' if end_date_found is false), Days Remaining (blank if end_date_found is false), and Notes (show 'Multiple possible dates found' if has_multiple_dates is true, otherwise blank). 4) Sort the table rows so contracts with the fewest days_remaining appear first, and contracts with no end date appear at the bottom. 5) Style the table with professional formatting: borders, header row with background color, padding, and readable fonts. Use inline CSS for email compatibility.
generate.reason: Requires semantic composition of an HTML email body with conditional content (empty-state message vs populated table), dynamic sorting logic, and professional styling — no template structure available

outputs.name: html_body
outputs.description: Complete HTML email body with summary header and styled table

outputs.name: summary_line
outputs.description: Short plain-text summary for reference (e.g., '3 contracts expiring soon, 1 missing end date')

steps.summary: Send the HTML contract expiration summary email to the specified recipients

meta.estimated_steps_per_run: Variable: 3 fixed steps + N loop iterations (one per Google Doc in Contracts folder) + 4 post-loop steps
key_assumptions: The 'Contracts' folder already exists in Google Drive
Contract documents are Google Docs (native format), not uploaded PDFs or other file types
End/expiration dates in contracts are expressed in recognizable date formats near standard labels
The read_file_content action with text/plain export provides sufficient text for date extraction from Google Docs
